channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from trusted sources worldwide

item.title: 🇺🇦 Drones Strike Historic Museum in Russian-Annexed Crimea
item.description: A drone attack damaged the roof of a historic museum in Sevastopol, with Russian-installed officials reducing nighttime train services for safety amid ongoing Ukrainian strikes.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Warns Gulf Nations of Responsibility to Prevent US-Israeli Strikes
item.description: Iran's foreign ministry declared that neighboring Gulf countries have a "legal and moral responsibility" to prevent American and Israeli attacks, as regional tensions escalate following US strikes.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi Concludes North Korea Visit with Renewed Signs of China's Influence
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping emerged from his first visit to North Korea in seven years with fresh assurances from Kim Jong Un, signaling deeper ties between the two nations amid regional uncertainties.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Strikes US Bases in Jordan and Gulf States in Retaliation
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards attacked a US base in Jordan and 21 other targets across the Gulf, marking a major escalation after American strikes near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland Demands Seat at Ukraine Peace Negotiations
item.description: Polish PM Tusk says Poland must be involved in discussions to end the Ukraine war, following Britain-France-Germany talks with Zelenskiy in London.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Wholesale Inflation Hits 3-Year High on Middle East Energy Shock
item.description: Japan's wholesale prices accelerated at the fastest pace in three years as energy costs from the Iran conflict broadened, strengthening the case for further rate hikes.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Bulgaria's New Government Ends Arms Support for Ukraine
item.description: Bulgaria's defence minister announced the country will no longer provide weapons to Ukraine and urged both sides to negotiate, a shift in Sofia's policy.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Charities Donated £28M to Illegal Israeli Settlements
item.description: Thirty-two charities in England and Wales have given at least £28m to Israeli settlements in the occupied territories, prompting calls for Charity Commission investigation.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan test-fires US-supplied HIMARS toward China in first public display
item.description: Taiwan staged its first use of the US-supplied HIMARS missile system in the Taiwan Strait as part of invasion-defense drills, a significant military demonstration amid ongoing tensions with Beijing.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Protesters torch vehicles and buildings in Belfast after refugee knife attack
item.description: Anti-immigrant protesters in Belfast, Northern Ireland, set fire to vehicles and buildings following a knife attack by a Sudanese refugee, marking a significant escalation in local tensions.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China prepares $295 billion AI infrastructure plan to challenge US dominance
item.description: China is preparing to spend around 2 trillion yuan ($295 billion) over five years on building data centers nationwide as Beijing intensifies its AI race with the United States.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Bill Gates to face US Congress questioning over Epstein links
item.description: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates will be questioned by US lawmakers over his relationship with late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, amid ongoing scrutiny of high-profile connections.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ IRGC Claims Strikes on US Bases in Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they targeted 21 US military sites including the Fifth Fleet HQ in Bahrain and Al-Azraq air base in Jordan, marking a significant escalation in the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇰🇼🛡️ Kuwait Intercepts Hostile Aerial Targets Amid Regional Escalation
item.description: Kuwait's army said its air defenses intercepted hostile aerial targets early Wednesday as tensions spike across the Gulf region following US strikes on Iran.

item.title: 🇯🇵💰 Japan's Largest Banks to Jointly Issue Stablecoins by March 2027
item.description: Japan's three largest banks announced plans to jointly issue stablecoins during the current fiscal year, marking a significant step in the country's digital currency adoption.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines earthquake death toll rises to 45, rescuers search rubble
item.description: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck southern Philippines, killing at least 45 people and displacing 32,000. Rescuers continue searching collapsed buildings in General Santos city for survivors.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Kakao workers stage strike demanding job security
item.description: Unionized workers at South Korean tech giant Kakao began a four-hour strike demanding job security and changes to the bonus system. The action highlights growing labor tensions in the country's tech sector.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey proposes 2035 global electricity target at climate summit
item.description: As host of this year's global climate summit, Turkey proposed targeting electricity to meet 35% of world energy demand by 2035, aiming to cut fossil fuel consumption. The initiative seeks to accelerate clean energy transition.

item.title: 🇵🇪 Peru election remains too close to call as markets rally
item.description: Peru's presidential election remains on a knife edge between conservative Keiko Fujimori and leftist candidate Sanchez, with vote counting potentially taking up to two weeks. Markets rallied amid the uncertainty.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US trade deficit narrows in April as exports surge to record highs
item.description: The US trade deficit narrowed in April as petroleum product exports hit record highs, potentially boosting second-quarter economic growth if the trend continues.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US helicopter pilots safe after going down in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump confirmed that two US Apache helicopter pilots were "fine" after their aircraft went down near the Strait of Hormuz, an incident that prompted retaliatory strikes against Iran.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China's May producer inflation hits nearly 4-year high as consumer prices rise
item.description: China's factory-gate prices rose at the fastest pace in nearly four years in May while consumer inflation ticked up, signaling building price pressures in the world's second-largest economy.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany pledges 300 million euros to Czech ammo initiative for Ukraine
item.description: Germany committed 300 million euros to the Czech ammunition initiative for Ukraine, joining allies in efforts to supply critical artillery rounds to Kyiv's forces.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Claims Downing US Helicopter, Vows Retaliation for Strikes
item.description: Iran claimed to have shot down a US Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. The US military confirmed an aircraft went down but said pilots were safe. Trump vowed retaliation for the incident.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Drone Strike Kills Russian General Overseeing Ammunition in Moscow
item.description: A car bomb attack in Moscow killed Russian General Davydov, who was overseeing ammunition supplies for the war in Ukraine, according to Ukrainian sources. The assassination marks another high-profile targeting of Russian military leadership.
